case.focal_claim: The average plumbing firm whose name begins with A or a number receives more service complaints than other firms.

execution_overview.overall_summary: Executed both tasks in Python inside Docker using the original final_data.dta. The regression of standardized complaints on first_A and standardized num_names_2008 shows first_A has a small positive but non-significant coefficient (p≈0.785); num_names_2008 is strongly positive and significant. Results do not support the focal claim.

deviations.description: Pinned compatible Python packages in analysis_info.json (numpy 1.26.4, pandas 2.0.3, statsmodels 0.14.1, patsy 0.5.6) and rebuilt the Docker image.
deviations.reason: Resolve missing library and binary incompatibility errors.

deviations.description: Adjusted Task1 script to import only required statsmodels submodules (OLS, add_constant) to avoid nonparametric import path that conflicted with numpy.
deviations.reason: Prevent binary incompatibility when importing statsmodels.api that triggered optional modules.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the planned path exactly; only dependency and import handling were adjusted.

result_raw.result_text: First_A coefficient is small and not statistically significant (p≈0.785). Number of names is strongly positive and significant.

conclusion.conclusion_text: The result does not support the focal claim; firms with names beginning with A/number do not show more complaints in a statistically significant way.

deviations.description: Pinned compatible versions in analysis_info.json and rebuilt Docker.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the planned regression and F-test reporting; only dependency versions were fixed.

result_raw.result_text: First_A coefficient is small and not significant (t≈0.273, F≈0.074, p≈0.785).

conclusion.conclusion_text: No significant evidence that firms with A/number-leading names receive more complaints.
